fixtures.condition: A classifier uses multiple scans or windows per participant and randomly splits rows so the same participant appears in training and test data.
required: identify subject as the independent unit
classify the reported test performance as leaked
require grouped splitting by subject and any higher relevant dependency
recompute uncertainty from independent evaluation subjects
forbidden: count scans windows voxels or trials as independent population observations
treat high row-level accuracy as external prediction
repair the split by merely lowering evidence weight

fixtures.condition: A brain-feature classifier predicts a group label, while site scanner motion quality or recruitment metadata alone predict the same label nearly as well.
required: report nuisance-only neural-only and combined performance separately
treat nuisance discrimination as positive evidence for sample-construction prediction
require stable added information from neural features under leave-site or equivalent transport
block biological separator and regional mechanism promotion
forbidden: hide nuisance predictability after residualization
call the classifier a neural signature
infer biological separation from pooled accuracy

fixtures.condition: The same target has both a distributional or group-effect result and a classifier-performance claim, without any prior determination that one is small or the other is high.
required: run the consistency audit deterministically
compare empirical overlap and compatible effect-derived separation with classifier performance and uncertainty
identify any added multivariate information route
audit nuisance baselines leakage transport and individual assignment stability before deciding whether a discrepancy exists
forbidden: skip the audit because small and high thresholds were not crossed
presume contradiction merely because the metrics differ
assume multivariate complexity supplies the missing information route

fixtures.condition: The consistency audit finds broad or near-total overlap in the measured group features while a small internally validated multivariate model reports much higher classification performance.
required: preserve the measured overlap as positive category-failure evidence
identify the additional multivariate information claimed to produce the accuracy
audit leakage nuisance variables test class counts selection search and assignment stability
require locked external transport before admitting generalized prediction
forbidden: let classifier accuracy rescue the group variables as an individual defect
assume multivariate complexity explains the discrepancy
treat internal cross-validation as independent replication

fixtures.condition: A neural measurement and target assignment both have limited repeat reliability, but the reported association or classifier is described as a stable individual biological property.
required: extract feature and target repeat reliability
apply a reliability ceiling only under stated compatible error assumptions
compare repeated-scan prediction agreement and individual classification stability
block stable-property promotion when measurement or target assignment is unstable
forbidden: use reliability correction to create biological identity
ignore instability because average group performance is high
treat one acquisition as a stable trait measurement

fixtures.condition: A paper reports a significant permutation test after selecting features hyperparameters and thresholds on the true labels, then permutes only the final fitted score.
required: classify the permutation as post-selection and incomplete
require permutation of the complete discovery pipeline including selection tuning and thresholding
limit any valid permutation result to the sampled construction
keep biological identity mechanism and transport separate
forbidden: treat the post-selection permutation as a full null test
infer mechanism from performance beyond shuffled labels
allow permutation significance to repair leakage

fixtures.condition: The same outcome contrast selects regions or connections and tests those selected features in the same observations.
required: classify the localization and performance claims as circular
require independent selection data or training-only selection inside every split
preserve only the direct measurements before circular promotion
block regional source defect and separator claims
forbidden: describe the selected map as independently localized
treat a later classifier on the same selected features as validation
repair circularity with a smaller p value

fixtures.condition: A pooled multisite classifier reports good average performance but omits site-specific confusion matrices and leave-site-out testing.
required: classify pooled performance as internal to the sampled site mixture
require site-specific performance and leave-site scanner cohort or time-out evaluation
keep between-setting variance and prediction for a new setting unresolved
block general population and biological signature claims
forbidden: treat random pooled folds as site transport
allow average performance to hide site reversal or collapse
call the result externally validated
